workItem.title: Honor provider Retry-After with bounded circuit jitter

## Context

The process-wide provider circuit truncated explicit Retry-After values to the 15-minute internal backoff ceiling and reopened without jitter. A real 2,123-second GitHub cooldown therefore caused an avoidable early probe and allowed concurrent runners to resume in lockstep.

## Expected outcome

Symphony waits for the complete normalized provider cooldown plus bounded positive jitter, preserves one process-wide circuit and does not shorten the provider window.

## Scope

- ProviderCircuit cooldown calculation
- Deterministic regression tests for long provider windows and jitter bounds
- Operational documentation

## Out of scope

- Changing game-api rate-limit classification
- Adding per-run provider circuits
- Changing GitHub credentials or provider quotas

## Acceptance criteria

- An explicit provider Retry-After longer than 15 minutes is not truncated to the internal backoff ceiling.
- A positive jitter is added without shortening the provider Retry-After window.
- Jitter is bounded to at most 10 percent and 30 seconds.
- Provider guidance has a documented defensive ceiling and the internal backoff remains bounded.
- Repeated callers share the existing circuit and do not multiply attempts.
- make -C elixir all passes with full coverage, Credo and Dialyzer.
